description: Look after a person, living thing, place, or fire through recurring practical acts. Tending a garden may involve watering and pruning; tending a sick person calls for different work. 'theama' (care) is the wider relation, while 'thiranu' (maintain) keeps something near a required condition or function.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 't' and release it into middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for stressed 'm', carry the voice through the nose, and open into rounded middle-height back 'o'. Keep the final middle-height front 'e' separate.
sound_symbolism: A small dental release leads to the held resonance of stressed 'mo', followed by one last open syllable. The measured sequence can cue attention that returns rather than a single intervention.
usage_notes: The person, place, or living thing receiving attention precedes 'temoe'. The surrounding clause can give the actual work or interval. For emotional or protective care without this recurring practical focus, 'theama' remains the broader choice.

pillars.solarpunk-values: The Solarpunk Manifesto calls its stewards people who tend gardens. 'temoe' grounds stewardship in work that returns to the same living place.

semantic_domains.activity: recurring practical attention to what needs looking after
semantic_domains.nature: regular work with a garden, animal, or other living thing
